trails.name: Rangihau Track
trails.description: This track descends from Pinnacles Hut to Rangihau Road end.

trails.name: Bell Track
trails.description: Combine this track with Tahuanui Track for an overnight circuit to Pāhautea Hut and Mount Pirongia.

trails.name: Whangamarino Historic Walk
trails.description: This short walk takes in the Whangamarino Redoubt and Te Teoteo's Pā – two key sites of conflict during the Waikato War of 1863-64. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Mahaukura Track
trails.description: This track takes in the Wharauroa and Mahaukura Lookouts on its way up to the summit of Mount Pirongia.

trails.name: Mangakara Nature Walk
trails.description: This loop walk passes through ancient forest with towering trees and a beautiful stream - with interpretation signs as well this is a great option for children.

trails.name: Ruapane Track
trails.description: Climb to a lookout on Mount Pirongia with excellent views across the Waikato Basin to the Kaimai Ranges.

trails.name: WEBB CREEK TO HYDRO CAMP TK
HYDRO CAMP TO PINNACLES HUT TK
Karamu W/Way Nth- Lookout to Old Mountai
Karamu W/Way Sth- O.M.R. to Limeworks Lo
PUKETUI WALK
trails.description: This flat track connects two road ends and provides good views of the Tairua River.

trails.name: Third Branch Track
trails.description: This track can be joined with Water Race Link Track and Puketi Walk to be completed as a loop.

trails.name: PAUANUI TO FLAT ROCK SHORT WALK
Hakarimata Rail Trail to Summit track
Tirohanga Track
trails.description: Walk up to Pāhautea Hut and Campsite via Mount Pirongia summit from where you might see Mount Taranaki or Mount Ruapehu on a clear day.

trails.name: Nikau Walk
trails.description: Follow a stream through beautiful native forest with swimming and picnicking opportunities. This track is great for children. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Paton Stream to Hihi Trig Track
Tarawaere to Waterfall & Billy Goat link
BILLY GOAT TO HIHI TRIG TT
WAIOMU KAURI  GROVE  WALK
trails.description: A 2-hour return walk allows you to visit one of the Coromandel Peninsula’s finest remaining kauri stands, Waiomu Kauri Grove.

trails.name: MT ELIZA MINE TK -N/S to THOMPSONS
Karaka to Crosbies Hut Tramping Track
MARATOTO ROAD TO GOLDEN CROSS T' TRACK
WENTWORTH FALLS WALK
trails.description: This track frollows an easy grade alongside the river to the Wentworth Falls.

trails.name: MOSS CREEK TRACK
trails.description: This track is a steep climb to Moss Creek Campsite.

trails.name: WAIRERE TK - WAIRERE FALLS TO CLAY RD
LODGE TO ROAD VIA TE RERE FALLS TK
TE PURU TRACK
trails.description: Te Puru Track joins with Tapu summit to Crosbies Hut Track to take you to Crosbies Hut.

trails.name: Hakarimata Walkway
trails.description: Walk the length of the Hakarimata Range and enjoy excellent views towards the coast and across the Waikato Basin. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Mangamuka Hut to North / South Track
Mangamuka Hut to Te Tuhi Track
Cookson Kauri Walk
trails.description: Walk to the magnificent Cookson kauri tree.

trails.name: Hakarimata Summit Track
trails.description: Climb to the summit of Hakarimata (374 m) and enjoy good views for the summit tower out towards the coast, across the Waikato Basin and down to Ruapehu. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Bluff Stream Kauri Loop Track
trails.description: This track has been closed to prevent the spread of kauri dieback.

trails.name: Broken Hills walking tracks
trails.description: The Broken Hills area has a number of walking tracks through old mining sites.

trails.name: Crown Track to Dickey's Flat
trails.description: Take this short walk through historic mining country through to the Dickey Flat Campsite.

trails.name: Daly's Clearing Loop Track
trails.description: A highlight along this track is the extensive groves of nīkau nestled under tall tawa, kohekohe and pukatea forest.

trails.name: Eliza Mine Loop Track
trails.description: Evidence of the past can be savoured today as trampers walk through the Eliza Mine Loop Track.

trails.name: Hakarimata Rail Trail
trails.description: This family walking and cycling track follows the route of the former Ngāruawāhia/Glen Massey railway to attractive stream cascades.

trails.name: Hakarimata Walkway
trails.description: Walk the length of the Hakarimata Range and enjoy excellent views towards the coast and across the Waikato Basin. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Hapuakohe Walkway
trails.description: The Hapuakohe Walkway can be done as a 2-day trek, split by Ohinewai Road. Smaller sections can be completed within a day. It's suitable for experienced trampers only.

trails.name: Henderson Tramline Loop Track
trails.description: This walk follows a branch of the tramline through the bush to the North South track, and returns along a separate tramline branch back to the road.

trails.name: Kaniwhaniwha: Kahikatea tree
trails.description: Walk to the tallest recorded native tree in New Zealand.

trails.name: Kaniwhaniwha: Nikau Walk and Kaniwhaniwha Caves
trails.description: Follow a stream through beautiful native forest or walk through a 20 metre limestone cave - with swimming and picnicking opportunities as well, these tracks are great for children.

trails.name: Karakariki Track
trails.description: With suspension bridges, a stream, a waterfall and a nice spot for a picnic, this track has plenty to keep kids entertained.

trails.name: Karamu Walkway
trails.description: Superb 360 degree views and impressive limestone bluffs are features of this walk.

trails.name: Karangahake Gorge Historic Walkway
trails.description: Situated at the base of the Coromandel Range, the Karangahake Gorge Historic Walkway follows the old railway line between Paeroa and Waihi, giving access to impressive remains from the mining and railway eras.

trails.name: Karangahake Windows Walk
trails.description: This short walk boasts beautiful scenery and spectacular mining remnants including disused gold mining tunnels - kids will love it!

trails.name: Kauri Loop Track
trails.description: This well formed track offers spectacular views, beautiful bush and one of the largest kauri trees in the Waikato.

trails.name: Leyland O'Brien Tramline Track
trails.description: Explore the old Leyland O’Brien tramline besides the headwaters of the Ngamuwahine River.

trails.name: Lindemann Loop Track
trails.description: There is a popular lookout through this track with scenic views over the northern Bay of Plenty.

trails.name: Maratoto - Golden Cross Track
trails.description: The track from Maratoto to Golden Cross takes you past an old mining area and a regenerating broadleaf/hardwood forest.

trails.name: Maratoto - Wentworth Crossing
trails.description: Experienced trampers can cross from Maratoto to Wentworth.

trails.name: Ngamarama/Te Tuhi Track
trails.description: A challenging east-west crossing of the Kaimai Ranges featuring stream crossings and historic tramways.

trails.name: Ngamuwahine Track
trails.description: This track leads from the end of Ngamuwahine Road and takes you to a junction with the Leyland O'Brien Tramline Track.

trails.name: Orokawa Scenic Reserve short walking tracks
trails.description: Take one of the short walks in the reserve or combine for a longer coastal walk. Get spectacular views of the Pacific Ocean and Waihi Beach,  see bays with  mature pōhutukawa trees leaning over a sandy beach, and walks though coastal forest which includes

trails.name: Pirongia summit via Bell & Tahuanui Tracks
trails.description: Combine these tracks for an overnight circuit to Pāhautea Hut and Mount Pirongia.

trails.name: Pirongia summit via Mahaukura and Tirohanga Tracks
trails.description: Choose a there-and-back track to the summit of Mount Pirongia, or combine the tracks for a circuit trip to Pāhautea Hut. There are climbing chains on difficult parts of the track.

trails.name: Rapurapu Kauri Track
trails.description: This track takes you through a stream several times and to swimming holes and kauri trees.

trails.name: Ruapane Lookout
trails.description: Climb to a lookout on Mount Pirongia with excellent views across the Waikato Basin to the Kaimai Ranges.

trails.name: Sentinel Rock Lookout Track
trails.description: A challenging climb to an isolated rock outcrop in the Kaimai Ranges that offers spectacular views across the valley.

trails.name: Te Rereatukahia Hut Track
trails.description: Grass clearings and an old fence are a legacy of days gone by when this section was highly modified by burning and farming.

trails.name: Te Tapui Loop Track
trails.description: A loop track follows through the bush and up to the summit, with a lookout tower and great views across to the Kaimai Ranges and Firth of Thames.

trails.name: Tuahu kauri walking tracks
trails.description: These short walks feature kauri trees and are great for children.

trails.name: Tuahu Track
trails.description: This is an east-west crossing of the Kaimai Ranges used since the 1890s linking Katikati and Te Aroha. It can be walked in either direction.

trails.name: Waiorongomai Valley tramping tracks
trails.description: Explore pack tracks and tramlines from gold mining days - the shorter option is suitable for children and follows New Zealand’s oldest known railway (1882-83) with the original rail still in place.

trails.name: Wairere Falls Track
trails.description: Wairere Falls Track weaves its way through moss covered rocks and tree roots. Large round boulders form natural pools and cascading waterfalls.

trails.name: Waitawheta Tramway
trails.description: Follow an old tramline (1898 - 1928) on a relatively flat track through the spectacular Waitawheta Gorge - with interpretation panels as well, it's a great option for kids.

trails.name: Waitengaue Track
trails.description: Enjoy walking along the former Waitengaue bush tramway on this track that takes you to Waitawheta Hut.

trails.name: Wentworth Valley walks
trails.description: Walk to the beautiful Wentworth Falls or to views of two mine shafts.

trails.name: Wharauroa and Mahaukura Lookouts
trails.description: Climb up to one of the best views from Mount Pirongia of Hamilton and Te Awamutu.

trails.name: Wharekirauponga Track
trails.description: The Wharekirauponga Track in Parakiwai Valley and the Royal Standard Goldmine area follows along an old horse-drawn tram track.

trails.name: Billygoat Circuit
trails.description: This track offers views of the surrounding countryside and evidence of the logging era.

trails.name: Kauaeranga Kauri Trail (Pinnacles Walk)
trails.description: This popular walk leads to Pinnacles Hut, and on to the summit of the Pinnacles with spectacular views of the Coromandel Peninsula. It can be completed as a day or overnight walk.

trails.name: Kauaeranga Valley short walks
trails.description: You'll find numerous short walks in the Kauaeranga Valley. Many of the tracks wind through regenerating bush where remnants of the area's logging history can still be seen.

trails.name: Aongatete walks
trails.description: Walks at Aongatete take you through pūriri and kohekohe forest and along the Aongatete Stream.

trails.name: Kaitarakihi Summit Track
trails.description: This track takes you to one of the distinctive peaks of the Coromandel ranges.

trails.name: Maratoto: Wires Track
trails.description: This well marked track gives lovely views across Maratoto and you can see remnants of the old telephone line.

trails.name: Mount Te Aroha tracks
trails.description: A series of walks up and around Mount Te Aroha offer stunning views and give access to the Kaimai Mamaku Conservation Park.

trails.name: Cambridge North Green Belt
trails.description: An easy walk along the north and west sides of the Cambridge Green Belt.

trails.name: Lake Ngaroto
trails.description: A lakeside track through natural bush surrounded by farmland. An medium difficulty track. Limited opportunities for rest stops.

trails.name: Leamington Walkway
trails.description: An easy track mainly along footpaths in the South and East side of the Cambridge Green Belt.An easy track mainly along footpaths in the South and East side of the Cambridge Green Belt.

trails.name: Mount William Walkway
trails.description: Enjoy the views from the summit of Mount William, just south of the Bombay Hills. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Oak Arboretum   Track 1
trails.description: An easy track through a reserve containing Oak Trees.

trails.name: Oak Arboretum   Track 2
trails.description: An easy track through a reserve containing Oak Trees.

trails.name: Oaklands Walkway
trails.description: An easy walk along the north of the Green Belt (Brian Mayo Reserve) and then alongside State Highway 1. Dogs are allowed off-lead.

trails.name:
Rotopiko Wetland Discovery Trail
Rotopiko Wetland Discovery Trail
Rotopiko Wetland Discovery Trail
trails.description: An easy stroll for all ages across open grassland with lake views, to a gravel and wide boardwalk around a hidden peat lake, and through a towering kahikatea forest. A highlight for families is the interactive wetland trail - play Eels and Ladders, Mudfish Scrabble, and find the fernbird's nest. Schools and other group trips by appointment.

trails.name: Saffron Walkway   Section 1
trails.description: An easy track through the north of the Cambridge Green Belt (Brian Mayo Reserve). Dogs are allowed off-lead for this section of the track.

trails.name: Saffron Walkway   Section 2
trails.description: An easy track following alongside State Highway 1.

trails.name: Settlers Track
trails.description: A medium track, sloping with some steps. The track is at water level and occasionally floods in winter so care should be exercised. There is a waterfall feature at the half way point of this track.

trails.name: Soldiers Track
trails.description: This track was constructed in 1907. A group of magnolias was planted near the southern end of the high-level bridge to commemorate the centenary of its construction.

trails.name: Te Awa River Ride (section 1)
trails.description: An easy to moderate walk that stretches from the Avantidrome to the Gas Light Theatre. Largely flat but with a few steeper sections.

trails.name: Te Awa River Ride (Section 2)
trails.description: An easy to moderate walk that stretches from Payne Park to the Waikato River and connects to Section 1 of the Te Awa River Ride. Track goes through farmland with sheep and has been planted with daffodils.

trails.name: Te Awa River Ride (section 3)
trails.description: An easy to moderate walk that stretches from Leamington to Lake Karapiro. Largely flat but steeper in sections.

trails.name: Te Koo Utu Tracks
trails.description: Track around bottom of the lake. An easy track where dogs are allowed off lead.
